byline: Evaluating Quality in Human-Robot Interaction · Applied Sciences (MDPI)
title: Human-Robot Interaction Quality Survey Surpasses 300 Citations
summary: The review article 'Evaluating quality in human-robot interaction: A systematic search and classification of performance and human-centered factors, measures and metrics towards an Industry 5.0' has reached the milestone of more than 100 citations on Google Scholar.
description1: The paper offers a systematic classification of the performance and human-centered factors, measures, and metrics used to evaluate the quality of human-robot interaction — a shared vocabulary and toolbox increasingly adopted by the HRI and Industry 5.0 research community.
highlight: Passing 100 citations reflects the growing recognition that human-centered evaluation is a prerequisite for the responsible deployment of robots alongside people.
